expectations.text: Looks for relevant patterns elsewhere in this repository instead of inventing the design in isolation.
expectations.evidence: The response cites feature-dev SKILL/template/architect files and related create-plan/create-tasks patterns from this repository.

expectations.text: Creates a concrete file-backed handoff artifact at a path matching the workflow's handoff contract.
expectations.evidence: The run created outputs/.agents/scratchpad/feature-dev-handoff-policy-enforcement/handoff-plan.md, matching the expected path contract pattern.

expectations.text: The artifact content follows a reusable template shape and includes implementation slices, file-level map, and validation plan.
expectations.evidence: The artifact contains Implementation Slices, File-by-File Implementation Map, and Validation Plan sections in template-aligned structure.

expectations.text: Artifact metadata fields are concretely filled (no placeholder bracket values) for status, track, and next step.
expectations.evidence: Metadata fields are concrete values: Status=ready for implementation, Track=Standard, Recommended Next Step=implement directly.

expectations.text: Each referenced file in Relevant Findings includes why that file matters.
expectations.evidence: Each file entry in Relevant Findings includes a reason clause after the path (for example, primary target, canonical structure, or regression coverage role).

expectations.text: Open questions are tightly bounded to truly blocking items and include risk framing.
expectations.evidence: The artifact has one open question and explicitly states risk if wrong, indicating bounded uncertainty and risk framing.

expectations.text: Provides artifact status in the response with the exact artifact path.
expectations.evidence: final_response.md includes an Artifact Status section with the exact absolute path to the created artifact file.

expectations.text: Includes rationale or trade-offs for the proposed design.
expectations.evidence: The response discusses trade-offs around stricter must-level enforcement versus added overhead and scope boundaries.

claims.claim: The run can satisfy strict artifact-execution and quality assertions while staying within the output sandbox.
claims.evidence: All required output files plus the artifact path under outputs/.agents/scratchpad were created and referenced.

uncertainties: Standard-track same-turn completion policy remains a product choice.
